title: Operation Cast Lead: 1,387 Palestinians Killed by Israel, 773 of Them Did Not Take Part in the Hostilities
strengths: Best status-classified direct-death series in the source set; explicitly separates non-combatants, combatants, police, and unresolved cases.
limitations: Police category is analytically awkward because B'Tselem would not classify all police killed at stations as legitimate military targets.
notes: Paraphrase: 1,387 Palestinians were killed; 773 did not take part in hostilities, 330 took part, 248 police were killed at police stations, and 36 remained unresolved.

title: Estimated Population in the Palestinian Territory Mid-Year by Governorate, 1997-2006
strengths: Official population benchmark for the Gaza Strip before the war.
limitations: Single mid-year point rather than daily war-period population.
notes: Paraphrase: the Gaza Strip population was about 1.4 million in mid-2008.

title: On the Eve of 2010, the Population of the Palestinian Territory is 4.0 Million
strengths: Official population benchmark for the Gaza Strip after the war.
limitations: Single mid-year point rather than daily war-period population.
notes: Paraphrase: the Gaza Strip population was about 1.5 million in mid-2009.

title: Hamas’s Military Capabilities in the Gaza Strip
author_or_org: The Washington Institute for Near East Policy
date_scope: Pre- and intra-war force structure context
strengths: Provides both a narrow core-combatant estimate and a broad force ceiling for Hamas military/paramilitary/police manpower.
limitations: Not a casualty source; broad upper bound includes police and paramilitary categories that do not map cleanly onto B'Tselem's combatant-death bucket.
notes: Paraphrase: Qassam Brigades had roughly 2,000 real combat forces, while Hamas could have as many as 15,000-16,000 combatants if military, paramilitary, and police are all counted.

title: Palestinian fatalities during Operation Cast Lead
pages_or_sections: PDF pp. 1-2, fatality breakdown table: 773 non-participants; 107 women over 18; 320 minor boys and girls under 18.
strengths: Direct episode-wide breakdown with explicit civilian status and explicit non-overlapping age/sex categories.
limitations: Adult population baselines are not provided; those must be brought in from demographic sources.
notes: This is the load-bearing fatality source for the bundle.

title: On the Eve of International Population Day 11/7/2009
pages_or_sections: PDF p. 1, demographic status section: Gaza population 1.5 million; 755 thousand males and 732 thousand females.
strengths: Official Gaza baseline close in time to the operation.
limitations: Provides all-female share but not under-18 or adult-female share.
notes: Used for total population and female share.

citation_short: PCBS child share via WAFA, Nov 2009
title: PCBS: Half of Palestinian Society under 18
pages_or_sections: Article context: children under 18 were 52.5% of Gaza Strip population in mid-2009.
strengths: Gives the exact under-18 Gaza share needed for the operation year.
limitations: This is a WAFA report of PCBS rather than a directly opened PCBS table.
notes: Used for the under-18 population share.

title: H.E. Dr. Awad, highlights the Palestinian children's situation on Palestinian Children's Day, 04/04/2024
pages_or_sections: Article context: Gaza under-18 population 1,067,986, including 544,776 males and 523,210 females.
strengths: Provides a Gaza-specific child sex split for deriving adult-female and adult-male baseline shares.
limitations: Later than the conflict; used as a demographic structure assumption rather than a direct conflict-year baseline.
